title: ArtifactManifest (v1)
description: The set of artifacts produced for a single Maestro flow run. Owned and emitted by Orchestra; written as manifest.json in the run's artifact root and consumed verbatim by the CLI and the cloud worker.

$schema.description: Stable HTTPS URL identifying this schema (published to the public maestro-schemas GCS bucket), so the manifest stays self-describing wherever it is moved, copied, or uploaded.

schemaVersion.description: Bumped only on a STRUCTURAL change to this file's shape, not for additive fields (those rely on the reader tolerating unknown properties).

entries.description: One element per artifact, or per homogeneous collection of artifacts. Omitted when empty.

ArtifactEntry.description: One artifact, or one homogeneous collection of artifacts.

format.description: Member format when the entry is homogeneous; omitted for a mixed collection.

relativePath.description: Path relative to the run root (the dir holding manifest.json), which is itself the zippable artifact bundle. Everything core makes sits directly under it: commands.json, logs/maestro.log (plus device logs and crash/ANR on worker/cloud), takeScreenshot/, startRecording/, the failure screenshot, the per-step screenshots/ folder, and screen-recording.mp4. A directory when count is set, otherwise a single file.

count.description: Omitted for a single file; set to the member count for a collection living under relativePath.

sizeBytes.description: Size of the file in bytes; omitted when unknown or for a collection.

metadata.description: Free-form string key/value pairs qualifying the artifact. metadata.source identifies the log origin for DEVICE_LOG: simulator | xctest | emulator. Omitted when empty.

ArtifactKind.description: What an artifact is, semantically. Superset across local + cloud; each environment emits the subset it produces. Wire form is the enum name in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

oneOf.description: A step PNG screenshot in the run-root screenshots/ folder. With captureStepScreenshots on (worker), one per executed command; otherwise only the failed command's. The failed step's file is identified via commands.json (the FAILED command's artifacts entry).
Output of the takeScreenshot command, under the run-root takeScreenshot/ folder.
The whole-run MP4 recording at the run-root screen-recording.mp4. Flag-gated; emitted by the worker, not the local CLI.
Output of the startRecording command, under the run-root startRecording/ folder.

The view hierarchy, captured per step as its own JSON artifact (run-root screen-hierarchy/ folder, one step-<N>.json per executed command) and referenced from that command's COMMAND_METADATA artifacts list. This is the only home of the hierarchy; it is no longer inlined in commands.json.

commands.json: per-command execution metadata (status, timing, evaluated command) plus a typed artifacts list ({type, path} objects, run-root-relative) referencing the files each command produced. The view hierarchy is NOT inline here: it is a separate SCREEN_HIERARCHY artifact, one per executed command, referenced from that command's artifacts list.
maestro.log: the scoped capture of maestro.* logger output for this flow run.
Raw device-side log output. metadata.source identifies the origin: simulator, xctest, or emulator.
An app crash report collected from the device.
An Android 'Application Not Responding' report collected from the device.
Other files the flow produced or pushed to the run output. A collection.
Reserved for AI-generated analysis of the run. Not emitted yet.

ArtifactFormat.description: Concrete on-disk format of an artifact's bytes. ZIP is a download view, not a kind, and so is absent here.
